title: Sonnets. IX
content:
Lady that in the prime of earliest youth,
    Wisely hath shun'd the broad way and the green,
    And with those few art eminently seen,
    That labour up the Hill of heav'nly Truth,
    The better part with Mary and with Ruth,
    Chosen thou hast, and they that overween,
    And at thy growing vertues fret their spleen,
    No anger find in thee, but pity and ruth.
    Thy care is fixt and zealously attends
    To fill thy odorous Lamp with deeds of light,
    And Hope that reaps not shame. Therefore be sure
    Thou, when the Bridegroom with his feastfull friends
    Passes to bliss at the mid hour of night,
    Hast gain'd thy entrance, Virgin wise and pure.
meaning:
John Milton's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Sonnets. IX"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
